hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), the truthful and truly-inspired, said, "Each one of you collected in the womb of his mother for forty days, and then turns into a clot for an equal period (of forty days) and turns into a piece of flesh for a similar period (of forty days) and then Allah sends an angel and orders him to write four things, i.e., his provision, his age, and whether he will be of the wretched or the blessed (in the Hereafter). Then the soul is breathed into him. And by Allah, a person among you (or a man) may do deeds of the people of the Fire till there is only a cubit or an arm-breadth distance between him and the Fire, but then that writing (which Allah has ordered the angel to write) precedes, and he does the deeds of the people of Paradise and enters it; and a man may do the deeds of the people of Paradise till there is only a cubit or two between him and Paradise, and then that writing precedes and he does the deeds of the people of the Fire and enters it
question: What is the process of human development in the womb according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer describes the process of human development in the womb as taught by the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah puts an angel in charge of the uterus and the angel says, 'O Lord, (it is) semen! O Lord, (it is now ) a clot! O Lord, (it is now) a piece of flesh.' And then, if Allah wishes to complete its creation, the angel asks, 'O Lord, (will it be) a male or a female? A wretched (an evil doer) or a blessed (doer of good)? How much will his provisions be? What will his age be?' So all that is written while the creature is still in the mother's womb
question: What role does an angel play in the development of a fetus according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the role of an angel in the development of a fetus according to Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Imran bin Husain:A man said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Can the people of Paradise be known (differentiated) from the people of the Fire; The Prophet (ﷺ) replied, "Yes." The man said, "Why do people (try to) do (good) deeds?" The Prophet said, "Everyone will do the deeds for which he has been created to do or he will do those deeds which will be made easy for him to do." (i.e. everybody will find easy to do such deeds as will lead him to his destined place for which he has been created)
question: How does the concept of predestination relate to human actions and their outcomes in Islamic belief?
verification_reason: The text explains the concept of predestination in relation to human actions and their outcomes, addressing the question of why people do good deeds.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No child is born but has the Islamic Faith, but its parents turn it into a Jew or a Christian. It is as you help the animals give birth. Do you find among their offspring a mutilated one before you mutilate them yourself?" The people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! What do you think about those (of them) who die young?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)
question: What is the concept of innate faith in Islam regarding newborns?

Do you find among their offspring a mutilated one before you mutilate them yourself?" The people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! What do you think about those (of them) who die young?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No woman should ask for the divorce of her sister (Muslim) so as to take her place, but she should marry the man (without compelling him to divorce his other wife), for she will have nothing but what Allah has written for her

verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about a woman's request for her husband's divorce from another wife.

hadith-eng: Narrated Usama:Once while I was with the Prophet (ﷺ) and Sa`d, Ubai bin Ka`b and Mu`adh were also sitting with him, there came to him a messenger from one of his daughters, telling him that her child was on the verge of death. The Prophet (ﷺ) told the messenger to tell her, "It is for Allah what He takes, and it is for Allah what He gives, and everything has its fixed time (limit). So (she should) be patient and look for Allah's reward
question: What is the significance of patience and seeking Allah's reward during times of loss and hardship?
verification_reason: The text provides an example of the Prophet's guidance on patience and seeking Allah's reward during times of loss and hardship.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:That while he was sitting with the Prophet (ﷺ) a man from the Ansar came and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! We get slave girls from the war captives and we love property; what do you think about coitus interruptus?" Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do you do that? It is better for you not to do it, for there is no soul which Allah has ordained to come into existence but will be created
question: What is the Islamic perspective on coitus interruptus and its implications on procreation?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about coitus interruptus and its implications on procreation.

hadith-eng: Narrated Hudhaifa:The Prophet (ﷺ) once delivered a speech in front of us wherein he left nothing but mentioned (about) everything that would happen till the Hour. Some of us stored that our minds and some forgot it. (After that speech) I used to see events taking place (which had been referred to in that speech) but I had forgotten them (before their occurrence). Then I would recognize such events as a man recognizes another man who has been absent and then sees and recognizes him
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) describe future events leading up to the Day of Judgment?
verification_reason: The answer describes the Prophet's speech about future events leading up to the Day of Judgment, matching the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Ali:While we were sitting with the Prophet (ﷺ) who had a stick with which he was scraping the earth, he lowered his head and said, "There is none of you but has his place assigned either in the Fire or in Paradise." Thereupon a man from the people said, "Shall we not depend upon this, O Allah's Apostle?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No, but carry on and do your deeds, for everybody finds it easy to do such deeds (as will lead him to his place)." The Prophet (ﷺ) then recited the Verse: 'As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah
question: What does Islamic teaching say about predestination and human effort in determining one's fate in the afterlife?
verification_reason: The answer explains the concept of predestination and human effort in determining one's fate in the afterlife, as mentioned in the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:We witnessed along with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) the Khaibar (campaign). Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) told his companions about a man who claimed to be a Muslim, "This man is from the people of the Fire." When the battle started, the man fought very bravely and received a great number of wounds and got crippled. On that, a man from among the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) came and said, "O Allah's Apostle! Do you know what the man you described as of the people of the Fire has done? He has fought very bravely for Allah's Cause and he has received many wounds." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "But he is indeed one of the people of the Fire." Some of the Muslims were about to have some doubt about that statement. So while the man was in that state, the pain caused by the wounds troubled him so much that he put his hand into his quiver and took out an arrow and committed suicide with it. Off went some men from among the Muslims to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Allah has made your statement true. So-and-so has committed suicide." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "O Bilal! Get up and announce in public: None will enter Paradise but a believer, and Allah may support this religion (Islam) with a wicked man
question: What lesson can be learned about the true nature of faith and its relation to entering Paradise?
verification_reason: The answer illustrates the concept that faith is not solely determined by actions, but also by one's intentions and character.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:There was a man who fought most bravely of all the Muslims on behalf of the Muslims in a battle (Ghazwa) in the company of the Prophet. The Prophet (ﷺ) looked at him and said. "If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, let him look at this (brave man)." On that, a man from the People (Muslims) followed him, and he was in that state i.e., fighting fiercely against the pagans till he was wounded, and then he hastened to end his life by placing his sword between his breasts (and pressed it with great force) till it came out between his shoulders. Then the man (who was watching that person) went quickly to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said, "I testify that you are Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)!" The Prophet (ﷺ) asked him, "Why do you say that?" He said, "You said about so-and-so, 'If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, he should look at him.' He fought most bravely of all of us on behalf of the Muslims and I knew that he would not die as a Muslim (Martyr). So when he got wounded, he hastened to die and committed suicide." There-upon the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A man may do the deeds of the people of the Fire while in fact he is one of the people of Paradise, and he may do the deeds of the people of Paradise while in fact he belongs to the people of Fire, and verily, (the rewards of) the deeds are decided by the last actions (deeds)
question: What lesson can be learned about the unpredictability of a person's final destiny in Islam?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the unpredictability of a person's final destiny by showing a brave warrior who fought on behalf of the Muslims, but ultimately committed suicide, yet the Prophet's statement suggests that his final actions determined his fate.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade vowing and said, "In fact, vowing does not prevent anything, but it makes a miser to spend his property
question: What is the Islamic perspective on making vows and their effectiveness in changing outcomes?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question of the Islamic perspective on making vows and their effectiveness.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (that Allah said), "Vowing does not bring to the son of Adam anything I have not already written in his fate, but vowing is imposed on him by way of fore ordainment. Through vowing I make a miser spend of his wealth
question: What is the significance of vows in relation to predestination and fate in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer text accurately addresses the significance of vows in relation to predestination and fate in Islam, as stated by the Prophet (ﷺ).

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Musa:While we were with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in a holy battle, we never went up a hill or reached its peak or went down a valley but raised our voices with Takbir. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came close to us and said, "O people! Don't exert yourselves, for you do not call a deaf or an absent one, but you call the All- Listener, the All-Seer." The Prophet (ﷺ) then said, "O `Abdullah bin Qais! Shall I teach you a sentence which is from the treasures of Paradise? ( It is): 'La haula wala quwata illa billah. (There is neither might nor power except with Allah)
question: What is the significance of raising one's voice with Takbir during a holy battle?
verification_reason: The answer text explains the significance of raising one's voice with Takbir during a holy battle, which is to acknowledge Allah's presence and power.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:That the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No Caliph is appointed but has two groups of advisors: One group advises him to do good and urges him to adopt it, and the other group advises him to do bad and urges him to adopt it; and the protected is the one whom Allah protects
question: What are the types of advisors a leader might encounter and how do they influence his decisions?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the types of advisors a leader might encounter and their influence on decisions.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I did not see anything so resembling minor sins as what Abu Huraira said from the Prophet, who said, "Allah has written for the son of Adam his inevitable share of adultery whether he is aware of it or not: The adultery of the eye is the looking (at something which is sinful to look at), and the adultery of the tongue is to utter (what it is unlawful to utter), and the innerself wishes and longs for (adultery) and the private parts turn that into reality or refrain from submitting to the temptation
question: What are the different forms of adultery mentioned in the teachings of Islam?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the different forms of adultery mentioned in the teachings of Islam, as mentioned in the Hadith.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:(regarding the Verse) "And We granted the vision (Ascension to the heavens "Miraj") which We showed you (O Muhammad as an actual eye witness) but as a trial for mankind.' (17.60): Allah's Apostle actually saw with his own eyes the vision (all the things which were shown to him) on the night of his Night Journey to Jerusalem (and then to the heavens). The cursed tree which is mentioned in the Qur'an is the tree of Az-Zaqqum
question: What is the significance of the Night Journey and Ascension in Islamic belief?
verification_reason: The answer text explains the significance of the Night Journey and Ascension as a trial for mankind, as mentioned in the Verse.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Adam and Moses argued with each other. Moses said to Adam. 'O Adam! You are our father who disappointed us and turned us out of Paradise.' Then Adam said to him, 'O Moses! Allah favored you with His talk (talked to you directly) and He wrote (the Torah) for you with His Own Hand. Do you blame me for action which Allah had written in my fate forty years before my creation?' So Adam confuted Moses, Adam confuted Moses," the Prophet (ﷺ) added, repeating the Statement three times
question: What is the significance of predestination in Islamic theology as illustrated by the interaction between Adam and Moses?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the concept of predestination by showing Adam's argument that his actions were predetermined by Allah, thus highlighting the significance of predestination in Islamic theology.

hadith-eng: Narrated Warrad:(the freed slave of Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba) Muawiya wrote to Mughira. 'Write to me what you heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying after his prayer.' So Al-Mughira dictated to me and said, "I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying after the prayer, 'None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone Who has no partner. O Allah! No-one can withhold what You give, and none can give what You withhold, and the fortune of a man of means is useless before You (i.e., only good deeds are of value)
question: What is the significance of acknowledging Allah's sole right to be worshipped in Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explicitly mentions the significance of acknowledging Allah's sole right to be worshipped, which is a fundamental concept in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Take refuge with Allah from the difficulties of severe calamities, from having an evil end and a bad fate and from the malicious joy of your enemies
question: What are the specific things one should seek refuge from according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning specific things one should seek refuge from.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:When taking an oath, the Prophet (ﷺ) very often used to say, "No, by Him Who turns the hearts
question: What does the phrase 'by Him Who turns the hearts' signify in the context of taking oaths?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing the Prophet's common phrase when taking an oath.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to Ibn Saiyad, "I have kept for you a secret." Ibn Saiyad said, "Ad-Dukh." The Prophet said, "Keep quiet, for you cannot go beyond your limits (or you cannot exceed what has been foreordained for you)." On that, `Umar said (to the Prophet (ﷺ) ), "Allow me to chop off his neck!" The Prophet said, "Leave him, for if he is he (i.e., Ad-Dajjal), then you will not be able to overcome him, and if he is not, then you gain no good by killing him
question: What does this narration suggest about the limits of human knowledge and power in relation to divine decree?
verification_reason: The narration suggests that humans have limitations in understanding and overcoming divine decree, as indicated by the Prophet's statement to Ibn Saiyad and the Prophet's response to Umar's suggestion.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:I asked Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) about the plague. He said, "That was a means of torture which Allah used to send upon whom-so-ever He wished, but He made it a source of mercy for the believers, for anyone who is residing in a town in which this disease is present, and remains there and does not leave that town, but has patience and hopes for Allah's reward, and knows that nothing will befall him except what Allah has written for him, then he will get such reward as that of a martyr
question: How can a plague be both a punishment and a mercy according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer explains how plague can be both a punishment and a mercy according to Islamic teachings, as it is a means of torture for non-believers but a source of mercy for believers who show patience and hope for reward.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) on the Day of (the battle of) Al-Khandaq, carrying earth with us and saying, "By Allah, without Allah we would not have been guided, neither would we have fasted, nor would we have prayed. O Allah! Send down Sakina (calmness) upon us and make our feet firm when we meet (the enemy). The pagans have rebelled against us, but if they want to put us in affliction (i.e., fight us) we refuse (to flee)." (See Hadith No. 430, Vol)
question: What is the significance of seeking Allah's guidance and support in times of difficulty?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the Prophet's words on seeking Allah's guidance and support in times of difficulty.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:We witnessed along with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) the Khaibar (campaign). Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) told his companions about a man who claimed to be a Muslim, "This man is from the people of the Fire." When the battle started, the man fought very bravely and received a great number of wounds and got crippled. On that, a man from among the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) came and said, "O Allah's Apostle! Do you know what the man you described as of the people of the Fire has done? He has fought very bravely for Allah's Cause and he has received many wounds." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "But he is indeed one of the people of the Fire." Some of the Muslims were about to have some doubt about that statement. So while the man was in that state, the pain caused by the wounds troubled him so much that he put his hand into his quiver and took out an arrow and committed suicide with it. Off went some men from among the Muslims to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Allah has made your statement true. So-and-so has committed suicide." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "O Bilal! Get up and announce in public: None will enter Paradise but a believer, and Allah may support this religion (Islam) with a wicked man
question: How does this narration illustrate the concept that appearances can be deceiving in assessing one's faith?
verification_reason: The narration illustrates that the man's bravery and wounds do not necessarily indicate his faith, as he ultimately commits suicide, showing that appearances can be deceiving.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:There was a man who fought most bravely of all the Muslims on behalf of the Muslims in a battle (Ghazwa) in the company of the Prophet. The Prophet (ﷺ) looked at him and said. "If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, let him look at this (brave man)." On that, a man from the People (Muslims) followed him, and he was in that state i.e., fighting fiercely against the pagans till he was wounded, and then he hastened to end his life by placing his sword between his breasts (and pressed it with great force) till it came out between his shoulders. Then the man (who was watching that person) went quickly to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said, "I testify that you are Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)!" The Prophet (ﷺ) asked him, "Why do you say that?" He said, "You said about so-and-so, 'If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, he should look at him.' He fought most bravely of all of us on behalf of the Muslims and I knew that he would not die as a Muslim (Martyr). So when he got wounded, he hastened to die and committed suicide." There-upon the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A man may do the deeds of the people of the Fire while in fact he is one of the people of Paradise, and he may do the deeds of the people of Paradise while in fact he belongs to the people of Fire, and verily, (the rewards of) the deeds are decided by the last actions (deeds)
question: How does the concept of final actions influence the understanding of one's fate in the afterlife?
verification_reason: The text explains how a person's final actions can determine their fate in the afterlife, contrary to their previous deeds.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (that Allah said), "Vowing does not bring to the son of Adam anything I have not already written in his fate, but vowing is imposed on him by way of fore ordainment. Through vowing I make a miser spend of his wealth
question: How does vowing influence a person's behavior towards spending wealth according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer explains how vowing influences a person's behavior towards spending wealth, as it is used by Allah to make a miser spend his wealth.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:That the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No Caliph is appointed but has two groups of advisors: One group advises him to do good and urges him to adopt it, and the other group advises him to do bad and urges him to adopt it; and the protected is the one whom Allah protects
question: How can a leader discern between good and bad advice according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a prophetic guidance on how a leader can discern between good and bad advice.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:I did not see anything so resembling minor sins as what Abu Huraira said from the Prophet, who said, "Allah has written for the son of Adam his inevitable share of adultery whether he is aware of it or not: The adultery of the eye is the looking (at something which is sinful to look at), and the adultery of the tongue is to utter (what it is unlawful to utter), and the innerself wishes and longs for (adultery) and the private parts turn that into reality or refrain from submitting to the temptation
question: How does Islam view the concept of inevitable sins and human nature?
verification_reason: The text accurately addresses the concept of inevitable sins and human nature as viewed in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:(regarding the Verse) "And We granted the vision (Ascension to the heavens "Miraj") which We showed you (O Muhammad as an actual eye witness) but as a trial for mankind.' (17.60): Allah's Apostle actually saw with his own eyes the vision (all the things which were shown to him) on the night of his Night Journey to Jerusalem (and then to the heavens). The cursed tree which is mentioned in the Qur'an is the tree of Az-Zaqqum
question: How is the vision of the Prophet Muhammad during the Night Journey described in Islamic texts?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes the Prophet Muhammad's vision during the Night Journey as a real experience shown to him by Allah as a trial for mankind.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Adam and Moses argued with each other. Moses said to Adam. 'O Adam! You are our father who disappointed us and turned us out of Paradise.' Then Adam said to him, 'O Moses! Allah favored you with His talk (talked to you directly) and He wrote (the Torah) for you with His Own Hand. Do you blame me for action which Allah had written in my fate forty years before my creation?' So Adam confuted Moses, Adam confuted Moses," the Prophet (ﷺ) added, repeating the Statement three times
question: How does the dialogue between Adam and Moses highlight the concept of accountability and divine decree?
verification_reason: The dialogue between Adam and Moses is used to illustrate the concept of accountability and divine decree, as Adam argues that his actions were predetermined by Allah.

hadith-eng: Narrated Warrad:(the freed slave of Al-Mughira bin Shu`ba) Muawiya wrote to Mughira. 'Write to me what you heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying after his prayer.' So Al-Mughira dictated to me and said, "I heard the Prophet (ﷺ) saying after the prayer, 'None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone Who has no partner. O Allah! No-one can withhold what You give, and none can give what You withhold, and the fortune of a man of means is useless before You (i.e., only good deeds are of value)
question: How does the concept of divine control over giving and withholding relate to human understanding of wealth and provision?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the concept of divine control over giving and withholding, relating it to human understanding of wealth and provision.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Take refuge with Allah from the difficulties of severe calamities, from having an evil end and a bad fate and from the malicious joy of your enemies
question: Why is it important to seek protection from the malicious joy of enemies?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question of seeking protection from the malicious joy of enemies.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:When taking an oath, the Prophet (ﷺ) very often used to say, "No, by Him Who turns the hearts
question: How frequently did the Prophet (ﷺ) use specific phrases when taking oaths?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about the Prophet's (ﷺ) behavior when taking oaths.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) said to Ibn Saiyad, "I have kept for you a secret." Ibn Saiyad said, "Ad-Dukh." The Prophet said, "Keep quiet, for you cannot go beyond your limits (or you cannot exceed what has been foreordained for you)." On that, `Umar said (to the Prophet (ﷺ) ), "Allow me to chop off his neck!" The Prophet said, "Leave him, for if he is he (i.e., Ad-Dajjal), then you will not be able to overcome him, and if he is not, then you gain no good by killing him
question: How does this narration illustrate the Prophet's approach to dealing with potential threats or adversaries?
verification_reason: The narration illustrates the Prophet's approach to dealing with potential threats or adversaries by advising calmness and restraint, rather than resorting to violence or aggression.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Aisha:I asked Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) about the plague. He said, "That was a means of torture which Allah used to send upon whom-so-ever He wished, but He made it a source of mercy for the believers, for anyone who is residing in a town in which this disease is present, and remains there and does not leave that town, but has patience and hopes for Allah's reward, and knows that nothing will befall him except what Allah has written for him, then he will get such reward as that of a martyr
question: What actions and mindset should a believer maintain during a plague to attain the reward of a martyr?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear description of the mindset and actions required to attain the reward of a martyr during a plague.

hadith-eng: Narrated Al-Bara' bin `Azib:I saw the Prophet (ﷺ) on the Day of (the battle of) Al-Khandaq, carrying earth with us and saying, "By Allah, without Allah we would not have been guided, neither would we have fasted, nor would we have prayed. O Allah! Send down Sakina (calmness) upon us and make our feet firm when we meet (the enemy). The pagans have rebelled against us, but if they want to put us in affliction (i.e., fight us) we refuse (to flee)." (See Hadith No. 430, Vol)
question: How does the concept of Sakina (calmness) play a role in facing challenges according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text describes the Prophet's invocation for Sakina (calmness) in the face of challenges, aligning with the concept's role in Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), the truthful and truly-inspired, said, "Each one of you collected in the womb of his mother for forty days, and then turns into a clot for an equal period (of forty days) and turns into a piece of flesh for a similar period (of forty days) and then Allah sends an angel and orders him to write four things, i.e., his provision, his age, and whether he will be of the wretched or the blessed (in the Hereafter). Then the soul is breathed into him. And by Allah, a person among you (or a man) may do deeds of the people of the Fire till there is only a cubit or an arm-breadth distance between him and the Fire, but then that writing (which Allah has ordered the angel to write) precedes, and he does the deeds of the people of Paradise and enters it; and a man may do the deeds of the people of Paradise till there is only a cubit or two between him and Paradise, and then that writing precedes and he does the deeds of the people of the Fire and enters it
question: What are the four things that an angel is ordered to write about a person before their birth in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer text directly mentions the four things that an angel is ordered to write about a person before their birth, which are provision, age, being wretched or blessed, and no other information is provided in the question.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas bin Malik:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah puts an angel in charge of the uterus and the angel says, 'O Lord, (it is) semen! O Lord, (it is now ) a clot! O Lord, (it is now) a piece of flesh.' And then, if Allah wishes to complete its creation, the angel asks, 'O Lord, (will it be) a male or a female? A wretched (an evil doer) or a blessed (doer of good)? How much will his provisions be? What will his age be?' So all that is written while the creature is still in the mother's womb
question: What aspects of a person's life are predetermined while they are still in the womb according to Islamic tradition?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the aspects of a person's life that are predetermined while in the womb, as mentioned by the Prophet.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Imran bin Husain:A man said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Can the people of Paradise be known (differentiated) from the people of the Fire; The Prophet (ﷺ) replied, "Yes." The man said, "Why do people (try to) do (good) deeds?" The Prophet said, "Everyone will do the deeds for which he has been created to do or he will do those deeds which will be made easy for him to do." (i.e. everybody will find easy to do such deeds as will lead him to his destined place for which he has been created)
question: How does the ease of performing certain deeds relate to one's ultimate destiny according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer explains the relationship between ease of performing deeds and one's ultimate destiny.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) ; was asked about the offspring of the pagans. He said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)
question: What does Islamic teaching suggest about the accountability of those who die before reaching the age of maturity?
verification_reason: The answer addresses the question about the accountability of those who die before reaching the age of maturity, implying that Allah knows their intentions and actions as if they had lived.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira: Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about the offspring of the pagans. He said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)
question: What is the significance of acknowledging Allah's knowledge in matters beyond human understanding?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about Allah's knowledge in matters beyond human understanding.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No child is born but has the Islamic Faith, but its parents turn it into a Jew or a Christian. It is as you help the animals give birth. Do you find among their offspring a mutilated one before you mutilate them yourself?" The people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! What do you think about those (of them) who die young?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)
question: What is the Islamic perspective on the fate of children who die at a young age?
verification_reason: The answer text provides a clear Islamic perspective on the fate of children who die at a young age, citing the Prophet's statement.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No child is born but has the Islamic Faith, but its parents turn it into a Jew or a Christian. It is as you help the animals give birth. Do you find among their offspring a mutilated one before you mutilate them yourself?" The people said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! What do you think about those (of them) who die young?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Allah knows what they would have done (were they to live)
question: What is the Islamic perspective on the fate of children who die young?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the fate of children who die young and provides a perspective from the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him)

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No woman should ask for the divorce of her sister (Muslim) so as to take her place, but she should marry the man (without compelling him to divorce his other wife), for she will have nothing but what Allah has written for her
question: What ethical considerations are highlighted in Islam concerning marriage and the treatment of co-wives?
verification_reason: The text addresses the ethical consideration of not causing harm to a co-wife by asking for her divorce.

hadith-eng: Narrated Usama:Once while I was with the Prophet (ﷺ) and Sa`d, Ubai bin Ka`b and Mu`adh were also sitting with him, there came to him a messenger from one of his daughters, telling him that her child was on the verge of death. The Prophet (ﷺ) told the messenger to tell her, "It is for Allah what He takes, and it is for Allah what He gives, and everything has its fixed time (limit). So (she should) be patient and look for Allah's reward
question: What advice did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) give regarding coping with the death of a loved one?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing the Prophet's advice to his daughter regarding coping with the death of her child.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Sa`id Al-Khudri:That while he was sitting with the Prophet (ﷺ) a man from the Ansar came and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! We get slave girls from the war captives and we love property; what do you think about coitus interruptus?" Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do you do that? It is better for you not to do it, for there is no soul which Allah has ordained to come into existence but will be created
question: What guidance does Islam provide regarding the treatment and rights of war captives?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the question about the treatment and rights of war captives, specifically in the context of coitus interruptus and the creation of souls.

hadith-eng: Narrated Hudhaifa:The Prophet (ﷺ) once delivered a speech in front of us wherein he left nothing but mentioned (about) everything that would happen till the Hour. Some of us stored that our minds and some forgot it. (After that speech) I used to see events taking place (which had been referred to in that speech) but I had forgotten them (before their occurrence). Then I would recognize such events as a man recognizes another man who has been absent and then sees and recognizes him
question: How can past prophetic teachings help in recognizing future events as they unfold?
verification_reason: The answer explains how past prophetic teachings can help in recognizing future events as they unfold, by providing a means of recognition and confirmation.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Ali:While we were sitting with the Prophet (ﷺ) who had a stick with which he was scraping the earth, he lowered his head and said, "There is none of you but has his place assigned either in the Fire or in Paradise." Thereupon a man from the people said, "Shall we not depend upon this, O Allah's Apostle?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No, but carry on and do your deeds, for everybody finds it easy to do such deeds (as will lead him to his place)." The Prophet (ﷺ) then recited the Verse: 'As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah
question: What is the significance of the Quranic verse 'As for him who gives (in charity) and keeps his duty to Allah' in understanding one's actions and their outcomes?
verification_reason: The answer text explains the significance of the verse in understanding one's actions and their outcomes, as it relates to the concept of predestination and the importance of doing good deeds.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:We witnessed along with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) the Khaibar (campaign). Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) told his companions about a man who claimed to be a Muslim, "This man is from the people of the Fire." When the battle started, the man fought very bravely and received a great number of wounds and got crippled. On that, a man from among the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) came and said, "O Allah's Apostle! Do you know what the man you described as of the people of the Fire has done? He has fought very bravely for Allah's Cause and he has received many wounds." The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "But he is indeed one of the people of the Fire." Some of the Muslims were about to have some doubt about that statement. So while the man was in that state, the pain caused by the wounds troubled him so much that he put his hand into his quiver and took out an arrow and committed suicide with it. Off went some men from among the Muslims to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Allah has made your statement true. So-and-so has committed suicide." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "O Bilal! Get up and announce in public: None will enter Paradise but a believer, and Allah may support this religion (Islam) with a wicked man
question: What does this story teach about the potential for Allah to use individuals, regardless of their personal righteousness, to support Islam?
verification_reason: The story illustrates that Allah can use individuals, regardless of their personal righteousness, to support Islam, as the Prophet's statement is later proven true.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:There was a man who fought most bravely of all the Muslims on behalf of the Muslims in a battle (Ghazwa) in the company of the Prophet. The Prophet (ﷺ) looked at him and said. "If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, let him look at this (brave man)." On that, a man from the People (Muslims) followed him, and he was in that state i.e., fighting fiercely against the pagans till he was wounded, and then he hastened to end his life by placing his sword between his breasts (and pressed it with great force) till it came out between his shoulders. Then the man (who was watching that person) went quickly to the Prophet (ﷺ) and said, "I testify that you are Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)!" The Prophet (ﷺ) asked him, "Why do you say that?" He said, "You said about so-and-so, 'If anyone would like to see a man from the people of the Fire, he should look at him.' He fought most bravely of all of us on behalf of the Muslims and I knew that he would not die as a Muslim (Martyr). So when he got wounded, he hastened to die and committed suicide." There-upon the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "A man may do the deeds of the people of the Fire while in fact he is one of the people of Paradise, and he may do the deeds of the people of Paradise while in fact he belongs to the people of Fire, and verily, (the rewards of) the deeds are decided by the last actions (deeds)
question: What does this narration teach about the relationship between outward actions and inner faith?
verification_reason: The narration teaches that outward actions do not necessarily reflect inner faith, as the brave warrior was actually a believer, and that the last actions of a person determine their ultimate fate.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Umar:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade vowing and said, "In fact, vowing does not prevent anything, but it makes a miser to spend his property
question: What are the potential consequences of vowing on a person's financial behavior in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer explains the consequence of vowing, which is related to financial behavior.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said (that Allah said), "Vowing does not bring to the son of Adam anything I have not already written in his fate, but vowing is imposed on him by way of fore ordainment. Through vowing I make a miser spend of his wealth
question: What does this narration imply about the nature of vows and their impact on a person's destiny?
verification_reason: The narration clearly states that vowing does not change one's fate, but rather is a test or a means to bring out one's character, in this case, making a miser spend his wealth.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Musa:While we were with Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) in a holy battle, we never went up a hill or reached its peak or went down a valley but raised our voices with Takbir. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) came close to us and said, "O people! Don't exert yourselves, for you do not call a deaf or an absent one, but you call the All- Listener, the All-Seer." The Prophet (ﷺ) then said, "O `Abdullah bin Qais! Shall I teach you a sentence which is from the treasures of Paradise? ( It is): 'La haula wala quwata illa billah. (There is neither might nor power except with Allah)
question: How does the phrase 'La haula wala quwata illa billah' relate to the concept of divine power and reliance on Allah?
verification_reason: Re-authenticated automatically from valid source match.
